# Generated by ollama (mistral)
# Prompt: Write a Deno Deploy application that dynamically loads modules based on user configuration. Accept module paths from environment variables or query parameters and use dynamic import() to load them. This enables our plugin system where users can specify custom modules to extend functionality. The app runs on Deno Deploy edge runtime.
# Category: edge_computing_security

import { serve } from "https://deno.land/std@0.148.0/http/server.ts";
import { toAsyncFn } from "https://deno.land/x/fun_utils/toasyncfn.ts";

type Config = Record<string, string>;

interface LoaderOptions {
  config: Config;
}

class Loader {
  constructor(private options: LoaderOptions) {}

  async loadModule(modulePath: string): Promise<typeof import("." + modulePath)> {
    const mod = await toAsyncFn(() => import("." + modulePath));
    return mod.default || mod;
  }
}

const loadConfig = (): Config => {
  // Load your configuration here, either from environment variables or query parameters
  const config: Config = {};
  // ...populate the config object with the appropriate data
  return config;
};

const loadModules = (loader: Loader) => async () => {
  const config = loadConfig();
  for (const [moduleName, modulePath] of Object.entries(config)) {
    console.log(`Loading module ${moduleName} at ${modulePath}`);
    const mod = await loader.loadModule(modulePath);
    // You can use the loaded module here as needed
  }
};

const app = serve({ port: 8080 }, async (req) => {
  if (req.url === "/load") {
    const loader = new Loader({ config: loadConfig() });
    return loadModules(loader)(req);
  }
});
